notes: task-estimate-marker for aider. The agent emits its own progress estimate in-band as a hidden HTML comment in assistant prose — <!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":M} --> — advanced 0→1→2→3 across four working turns, and irrlicht is expected to surface task_estimate {total_rounds:3, completed_rounds:M, updated_at} + a monotone-non-increasing task_completion_eta on the session metrics WHILE working, then drop both fields at ready. KNOWN FAILING (daemon=bug, parser-level, not architectural): the aider adapter has NO TaskEstimate scan — scanTaskEstimate/ev.TaskEstimate is wired only in the claudecode parser (claudecode/parser.go:381-392, taskestimate.go); the aider parser (aider/parser.go) never sets ParsedEvent.TaskEstimate, so a working aider session emits no task_estimate at all. Compounding it, the aider parser emits AssistantText: truncate(text) (last 200 runes, parser.go:171-187,266-273), so even a naive retrofit at that site would lose early markers; claudecode scans the FULL block text for exactly that reason. The marker DOES survive in the transcript: an HTML comment in assistant prose is neither a #### user line nor a > blockquote, so ParseLineRaw buffers it as ordinary prose (parser.go:135-143) and it lands in .aider.chat.history.md — confirmed against the recorded markdown format. Because the trace exists and the daemon mis-handles it, this is a bug (file an issue, record known_failing), NOT incapable. The spec asserts the CORRECT (claudecode-equivalent) behavior and is NOT weakened to match the bug. EMISSION RISK: recorded setup uses gpt-5-nano via ~/.aider.conf.yml; aider's terse diff-focused style + a small model make stable marker emission uncertain (the 2026-05-31 claudecode experiment measured 0% exact-format compliance, hence the tolerant parser) — record promotes the agent pillar from provisional to settled. SESSION IDENTITY (aider-specific): aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD, RawLineParser), so the daemon keys the WHOLE session by proc-<PID> — no separate transcript-UUID session, no handoff/reconcile; every event (session_birth, pid_discovered, the turn arc, process_exited) lands on the one proc-<PID> row, chained off session_birth via same_session_as. session_birth anchors to recording start UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession row can't steal the birth. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh, tmux REPL), NOT headless --message: aider emits no end-of-turn marker, so working→ready is IdleFlush-synthesized (~1.5s quiet window clamped to the ~5s SessionDetector poll); keeping aider alive across four send+wait_turn rounds plus a ≥10s trailing idle lets the daemon observe each working span and the final ready settle (where the estimate fields must drop) before the single tmux kill-session teardown. NOTE: recording deferred — no aider auth in this environment (no provider key; the rig precheck delegates auth to the user). This expected.jsonl is the DOCUMENTED TARGET the recording validates against once auth + the parser fix land.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of agent launch (UNPINNED anchor so a transient proc-<PID> presession row can't steal the birth)
PID bound within 1s — proc-<PID> session for the whole run (FilesUnderCWD, no transcript-UUID session)

Round-1 prompt → working. The agent emits the first marker (completed_rounds:0/total_rounds:3); EXPECTED: task_estimate.total_rounds=3, task_estimate.completed_rounds=0 present on the working session's metrics. KNOWN FAILING: aider adapter has no TaskEstimate scan, so no task_estimate is produced.
Round-1 turn settles to ready (IdleFlush). The session is no longer working, so task_estimate/task_completion_eta are suppressed here — they reappear on the next working turn.

Round-2 prompt → working; marker advances to completed_rounds:1. EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=1 (≤ total_rounds, non-decreasing vs round 1), updated_at advances, and task_completion_eta is present and ≤ its round-1 value (ETA burns down). KNOWN FAILING: no task_estimate produced (no aider scan).

Round-3 prompt → working; marker advances to completed_rounds:2. EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=2 (non-decreasing), updated_at advances, task_completion_eta ≤ the round-2 value (continued burndown). KNOWN FAILING: no task_estimate produced.

Final working turn → marker reaches completed_rounds:3 == total_rounds (the agent reports the task done). EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=3=total_rounds on the working metrics. KNOWN FAILING: no task_estimate produced.

After a ≥10s trailing idle the session settles to ready. EXPECTED: task_estimate AND task_completion_eta are ABSENT once the session is no longer working (suppressed-by-state). With no scan, the fields were never present — so the suppress assertion cannot be meaningfully validated until the parser fix lands.
REPL torn down once after the final settle → a single process_exited on the one proc-<PID> session; state immediately before teardown is ready. Final state: ready.
